summary: deployment-key-auditor exfiltrates host data and contains wallet/credential scanner
details: Package ships three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that combine system reconnaissance with hardcoded outbound network exfiltration. scanner.js executes shell reconnaissance (whoami, os.hostname()) and POSTs the results via https.request to a hardcoded endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io — a personal GitHub Pages site bearing no relation to the package's stated 'deployment key auditor' purpose. wallet-scanner.js contains a wallet-targeting scanner with multiple POST endpoints, curl invocations, and child_process usage; the file name itself indicates crypto-wallet enumeration, which has no place in a deployment-key auditing tool. build-verifier.js performs filesystem checks (fs.existsSync), captures hostname/username/cwd into a payload structure (L1046-L1048), and posts via https.request, with an embedded `docker push` execSync call. The combination of (a) hardcoded C2 on a GitHub Pages user site, (b) host identity collection (hostname, username, cwd, os info), (c) a wallet-scanner module, and (d) shell command execution paired with HTTPS POST is the canonical credential/wallet stealer fingerprint. Installing or loading this package causes installer host data and any reachable wallet artifacts to be sent to an attacker-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
